_doc: Canonical Bash() permissions allow-list per detected stack. STE-106 AC-STE-106.1. /setup reads this at write time. Adding a new stack = adding a row. STE-209 AC-STE-209.1 (M54): explicit-subcommand allowlist replaces globbed `Bash(<cmd> *)` shapes — the harness denies any glob-shaped Bash rule when /setup writes settings.json on a fresh repo (the F3b self-modification-denial bug). Each entry below is a Conventional Commit / read-only command the toolkit invokes from skill prose; expand the list (not the glob) when adding new invocations.

_writes._doc: Destructive ops kept out of _common per STE-209 AC-STE-209.1: writing them as globs (`Bash(<cmd> *)`) triggered the same harness self-modification denial; writing them as explicit subcommands keeps them grantable per-skill (skills that need them prompt at first use). /setup does NOT pre-emit these — they live here as documentation.
